entries.forbidden_reading: crypto checks prove the trust root is legitimate
entries.statement: Cryptographic verification depends on correctness of the crypto library and implementation.

entries.forbidden_reading: algorithm acceptance proves key legitimacy or signer authority
entries.statement: Accepted cryptographic primitives and algorithms are assumed sound for the declared purpose.

entries.forbidden_reading: signature verification proves the private key was not compromised
entries.statement: Key management and private-key custody are assumed outside SPIRA.

entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA independently proves the key is legitimate
entries.statement: Legitimacy of a declared signing key is assumed from the declared trust root.

entries.forbidden_reading: a key accepted for one scope is universal
entries.statement: The declared signing key is assumed to be in-scope for the bounded purpose and payload class.

entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves the issuer is honest
entries.statement: Legitimacy of the identity issuer or registry is assumed from the declared identity root.

entries.forbidden_reading: identity binding proves signer authority
entries.statement: Credential-to-identity binding correctness depends on the declared issuer and identity namespace.

entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves the policy source is legitimate
entries.statement: Legitimacy of the authority-policy source is assumed from the declared authority root.

entries.forbidden_reading: policy lookup proves the policy is correct
entries.statement: Correctness of the authority policy itself is assumed, not proven.

entries.forbidden_reading: a fresh policy is necessarily correct
entries.statement: Policy freshness is assumed according to the declared freshness policy and clock source.

entries.forbidden_reading: revocation check proves the source is honest
entries.statement: Honesty and correctness of the revocation source are assumed from the declared root.

entries.forbidden_reading: stale or unreachable revocation data may be treated as valid
entries.statement: Revocation freshness depends on the declared freshness policy and clock source.

entries.forbidden_reading: unknown revocation status can support sufficiency
entries.statement: Unknown revocation status is not evidence of non-revocation.

entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves clock correctness
entries.statement: All freshness, validity-window, revocation, and attestation-time checks depend on declared clock trust.

entries.forbidden_reading: timestamp comparison proves real-world time correctness
entries.statement: Clock-source identity and freshness are assumed according to the declared trust root.

entries.forbidden_reading: isolation occurred or isolation is proven
entries.statement: Isolation execution truth is delegated to the declared attestation authority; SPIRA does not verify isolation occurred.

entries.forbidden_reading: a signed attestation proves the runner was honest
entries.statement: Honesty of the runner or observer producing the attestation is assumed.

entries.forbidden_reading: SPIRA proves OS, runtime, or container isolation correctness
entries.statement: Operating system, container runtime, and isolation substrate behavior are assumed outside SPIRA.

entries.forbidden_reading: declared root means legitimate root
entries.statement: Trust roots are declared inputs, not validated or proven by SPIRA.

entries.forbidden_reading: sufficient means unconditional authorization or permission to sever
entries.statement: TRUST_SUFFICIENT_UNDER_DECLARED_ROOTS is conditional on declared roots and this ledger.

entries.forbidden_reading: chain validated means assumptions disappeared
entries.statement: A validated chain of trust still exposes every trusted link as an assumption ID.

entries.forbidden_reading: assessment output authorizes or executes severance
entries.statement: Phase 2 assessment is not execution and not severance authorization.

stability_rule: IDs are never deleted silently; removing or weakening an assumption requires a new ledger version and review.
